title: About
text: HI! I’m Gavyn, a student at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln. I love the sports data that most don’t talk about and I love to share it!

title: Who is Punter University?
text:
Code
library(tidyverse)


── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.1.6
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
✔ purrr     1.2.1     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors


Code
library (cfbfastR)
library(dplyr)




Code
punt_2018_2025 <- read_csv("data/2018_2025_ncaa_punts_pbp.csv")


Warning: One or more parsing issues, call `problems()` on your data frame for details,
e.g.:
  dat <- vroom(...)
  problems(dat)


Rows: 83997 Columns: 362
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr   (42): pos_team, def_pos_team, play_type, play_text, penalty_detail, ho...
dbl  (200): year, week, id_play, game_id, game_play_number, half_play_number...
lgl  (118): penalty_1st_conv, home_EPA_rush, away_EPA_rush, home_EPA_pass, a...
dttm   (2): wallclock, start_date

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.




Code
good_punts <- punt_2018_2025 |> 
  mutate(
    in20 = ifelse(yards_to_goal >= 80, 1, 0),
    in10 = ifelse(yards_to_goal >= 90, 1, 0),
    in5  = ifelse(yards_to_goal >= 95, 1, 0)
  )


Aw man, punts? Seriously? Who even cares about punting? Well, punting is one of the most fundamental parts of the game of football. Field position and pinning your opponents deep is one of the most overlooked aspects of the game.
Sure, points are good too, but who doesn’t love watching their team have a great punt and the gunners running down and pinning them at the 1-yd line. And who doesn’t love being able to see these punters trought off the field happy as could be?
Statistics show that teams that punt more often don’t necessarily have the best punters. According to statistics from NCAA play-by-play data, there is no correlation between the teams that are punting more often and the teams pinning their opponents deep.


Code
good_punts |>
  filter(year >= 2020) |>
  group_by(pos_team) |>
  summarise(
    in20 = sum(in20, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_punts = n()
  ) |>
  mutate(
    pct_in20 = in20 / total_punts
  ) |>
  filter(total_punts >= 40) -> newtall

ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    data = newtall,
    aes(x = total_punts, y = pct_in20)
  ) +
  geom_smooth(
    data = newtall,
    aes(x = total_punts, y = pct_in20),
    method = "lm"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Do Teams That Punt More Pin Opponents Inside the 20 More Often?",
    subtitle = "Relationship between total punts and inside-the-20 percentage (since 2020)",
    x = "Total Punts",
    y = "Percentage of Punts Inside the 20",
    caption = "Author: Gavyn Klommhaus | Data: NCAA play-by-play punts (2020–2025)"
  )


`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'









But the real question is : Who is Punter University?
Within the previous five years, Punter University has been Eastern Kentucky. Eastern Kentucky has stood out within the last five years as having the highest percentage of punts that were downed within the 20-yd line.


Code
good_punts |>
  filter(year >= 2020) |>
  filter(
    home_team_conference == "SEC" |
    home_team_conference == "Big Ten" |
    home_team_conference == "Big 12" |
    home_team_conference == "ACC"
  ) |>
  group_by(pos_team) |>
  summarise(
    in20 = sum(in20, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_punts = n(),
    pct_in20 = in20 / total_punts
  ) |>
  filter(total_punts >= 35) |>
  arrange(desc(pct_in20)) |>
  slice_head(n = 20) |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_bar(
    aes(
      x = reorder(pos_team, pct_in20),
      weight = pct_in20
    )
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Best College Football Punting Teams Since 2020",
    subtitle = "Ranked by percentage of punts inside the 20-yard line",
    x = "Team",
    y = "Punts Inside the 20 Rate",
    caption = "Author: Gavyn Klommhaus | Data: NCAA play-by-play punts (2020–2025)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
